**Share the wall, stretch the wall.** Cork Board 1.1.0 adds a shareable board format and full layout control.

## New

- **Share Wall (HTML)** — one exported file that does both jobs. Anyone can open it in any browser and **print the whole board** to pin up for real — no app needed. And any Cork Board user can **import that same file** (Export → Import) to load the full project and keep working. Find it under **Export → Share Wall (HTML)**.
- **Rows wall** — a new **Wall: Columns / Rows** toggle in the board bar. Keep your acts as vertical columns and scroll sideways, or stretch the board horizontally so each act runs as a strip across the wall — like a real cork wall — and scroll down. Drag-and-drop works in both layouts.
- **Personal layout defaults** — your surface (Cork / Paper / Midnight), card size (S / M / L), and wall layout are remembered automatically and greet you on every launch.
- **Import** now accepts both project JSON files and Share Wall HTML files.

## macOS — easiest install (recommended)

Open **Terminal** (⌘-Space, type "Terminal") and paste this one line — it downloads the universal build (Apple Silicon & Intel), installs it to Applications, and opens it, with **no security warnings**:

```
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wassermanproductions/cork-board/main/install.sh | bash
```

## Which file do I download?

| Your computer | Download | After downloading |
|---|---|---|
| **Mac** (either kind) | *use the one-line installer above*, or `CorkBoard-macOS-universal.dmg` | See the unsigned-app note below |
| **Windows** | `CorkBoard-Setup.exe` (or the portable `CorkBoard-Windows-x64.zip`) | If SmartScreen appears: **More info → Run anyway** |
| **Linux** | `CorkBoard-Linux-x86_64.AppImage` (or the `.tar.gz`) | AppImage: `chmod +x` it first |

## macOS: "Cork Board is damaged and can't be opened"

If you downloaded the `.dmg` in your browser instead of using the installer above, macOS quarantines it and shows this misleading message. The app is **not** damaged — these builds just aren't Apple-notarized yet. Fix in 10 seconds:

1. Open the DMG and drag **Cork Board** into **Applications**.
2. In **Terminal**, paste:
   ```
   xattr -cr "/Applications/Cork Board.app"
   ```
3. Open Cork Board from Applications — it launches normally from now on.
